turns.user_message: I used to just brush off that heavy knot in my chest, but lately it sits right there like a stone. It starts out small in the morning and just refuses to loosen up as the day goes on. I keep telling myself it is just a normal reaction to a rough week, but the weight feels completely out of place. I try to push through it like I always do, yet I end up feeling completely drained by simple tasks. It is frustrating because I know I am making a bigger deal out of nothing.

That tightness in my ribs has stopped being a passing annoyance and turned into a constant reminder of how often I drop the ball. I catch myself replaying every little misstep over and over until the physical ache actually matches the mental exhaustion. It feels like I am carrying around a backpack full of bricks that I somehow keep adding to myself. I try to shake it off and act normal, but the heaviness just anchors me to the floor. I know I should just let it go, but I keep punishing myself for not being better.

The pressure behind my sternum has become so suffocating that I barely recognize the person looking back at me in the mirror anymore. I am convinced that every single mistake I make is just proof that I am fundamentally broken, and the weight of it crushes my chest until I can barely breathe. I try to convince myself that people would understand, but deep down I know I am just fooling myself with cheap excuses. I carry this heavy shame everywhere I go, and it feels like I will never be able to put it down. It is exhausting to live with the constant knowledge that I am the problem.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This used to not bother the speaker.
